source_docs.title: Second-Strand Synthesis-Based Massively Parallel scRNA-Seq Reveals Cellular States and Molecular Features of Human Inflammatory Skin Pathologies

oligos.name: Barcoded Bead SeqB (mRNA capture bead)
oligos.role: Bead-bound mRNA capture / RT primer; carries the cell barcode, UMI and poly(dT) capture tail

components.name: Bead-Linker
components.role: 5' attachment to the mRNA capture bead via a proprietary linker (sequence not printed)

oligos.notes: Printed as 5'-Bead-Linker--TTTTTTTAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACJJJJJJJJJJJJNNNNNNNNTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT-3'. J = 12-nt cell barcode, N = 8-nt UMI. ChemGenes MACOSKO-2011-10 (Drop-seq) beads. Read 1 can occasionally be 21 bp depending on bead lot.

oligos.name: Template Switching Oligo (TSO)
oligos.role: Template-switch oligo; appends the SMART sequence to the 3' end of first-strand cDNA during RT
oligos.notes: Printed as AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGAATrGrGrG; the 3' rGrGrG ribonucleotides pair with the CCC overhang added by the reverse transcriptase's terminal transferase activity.

oligos.name: dN-SMART Randomer (dN-SMRT)
oligos.role: Randomly priming second-strand synthesis primer carrying the SMART sequence in the 5' orientation
oligos.notes: The SMART sequence (AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGA) is 5' of a random-octamer priming region (NNNGGNNNB; B = C/G/T). Extended by Klenow exo- to make the SMART-tagged second strand.

oligos.name: SMART PCR Primer / IS PCR Primer (TSO_PCR)
oligos.role: Single primer for whole-transcriptome amplification (WTA); both ends of the dsDNA carry the SMART sequence
oligos.notes: Called SMART PCR Primer in the master protocol and IS PCR / ISPCR primer in STAR Methods; identical sequence.

oligos.name: New-P5-SMART PCR Hybrid Oligo (P5-TSO_Hybrid)
oligos.role: Library PCR primer 1 (P5 side); adds the P5 flow-cell adapter onto the SMART/bead end during Nextera library PCR

components.name: Seq-Well P5-SMART hybrid linker
components.role: Seq-Well-specific spacer bridging P5 to the SMART handle (also the 5' of the custom Read 1 primer)

components.name: SMART sequence
components.role: anneals to the SMART handle on the WTA product

oligos.notes: Printed as AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACGCCTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT*A*C; the 3'-terminal A*C are phosphorothioate-protected (*) in the ordered oligo (folded to AC here).

oligos.name: Nextera N7XX Oligo (i7)
oligos.role: Library PCR primer 2 (i7 side); adds the i7 sample index and P7 adapter at the tagmented (Nextera) end

components.name: Nextera i7 handle (s7)
components.role: anneals to the Nextera mosaic-end handle on the tagmented fragment

oligos.notes: Ordered from Illumina as the Nextera XT N7xx (N70x) index primer; the protocol names it but does not print the sequence. Constant P5/P7/handle regions shown are the standard Nextera N7xx adapter; exact index barcodes are supplied by the Nextera XT kit.

oligos.name: Custom Read 1 Primer (Read_1_Custom_SeqB)
oligos.role: Custom Read 1 sequencing primer; anneals to the P5/SMART end so Read 1 begins at the cell barcode
oligos.notes: Spiked in on NextSeq/NovaSeq runs (loaded at 2.2 pM). Read 1 = 20 bp (12-nt cell barcode + 8-nt UMI).

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell binding adapter (5' end of the final library), delivered via the New-P5-SMART hybrid oligo
oligos.notes: Verified constant; present as the 5' segment of the New-P5-SMART PCR hybrid oligo.

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell binding adapter (3' end of the final library), delivered via the Nextera N7xx oligo
oligos.notes: Verified constant; appears at the final-library 3' end as its reverse complement ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG.

final_library.source_label: Seq-Well S3 final tagmented library (Hughes et al., Immunity 2020)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC GCCTGTCCGCGG AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC [CELL_BARCODE:12] [UMI:8] TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT CCGAGCCCACGAGAC [SAMPLE_INDEX:8] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG

evidence.locator: Seq-Well S3 final tagmented library (Hughes et al., Immunity 2020)

library_generation.title: mRNA capture on barcoded bead (poly-dT priming)
library_generation.summary: Polyadenylated mRNA hybridizes to the poly(dT) tail of the bead-bound barcode oligo in each sealed nanowell.
library_generation.note: Cells and beads are co-confined in the sealed nanowell array; lysis releases mRNA that is captured by the terminal poly(dT)30 of the SeqB bead oligo.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription + template switching (Maxima H-RT)
library_generation.summary: Maxima H- RT extends the poly(dT) into first-strand cDNA and templates switches onto the TSO, appending the SMART handle to the cDNA 3' end.
library_generation.note: Terminal transferase activity adds a 3' CCC overhang; the TSO rGrGrG anneals and is copied, so first-strand cDNA carries the SMART handle at both ends.

library_generation.title: Exonuclease I digestion
library_generation.summary: Exonuclease I removes unextended single-stranded capture/RT primers before second-strand synthesis.
library_generation.note: Cleans up excess ssDNA primer; the bead-bound first-strand cDNA is unaffected.

library_generation.title: mRNA denaturation (0.1 M NaOH)
library_generation.summary: 0.1 M NaOH strips the RNA template, leaving single-stranded first-strand cDNA on the bead.
library_generation.note: Freshly made 0.1 M NaOH denatures the mRNA-cDNA hybrid so a new strand can be primed.

library_generation.title: Templated second-strand synthesis (Klenow exo- + dN-SMRT)
library_generation.summary: The dN-SMRT random primer anneals along the first strand and Klenow exo- synthesizes a SMART-tagged second strand.
library_generation.note: This is the S3 step: it recovers cDNA molecules that failed template switching by appending the SMART handle to the second strand, so both ends of the dsDNA carry SMART.

library_generation.title: Whole-transcriptome amplification (SMART/IS PCR primer)
library_generation.summary: A single SMART PCR primer amplifies the double-stranded cDNA because both ends carry the SMART handle.
library_generation.note: KAPA HiFi PCR (4 + 9-12 cycles) yields WTA cDNA flanked by the SMART sequence on both ends (avg 0.7-2 kb).

library_generation.title: Nextera XT tagmentation
library_generation.summary: Tn5 fragments the WTA cDNA and inserts Nextera mosaic-end adapters.
library_generation.note: Only fragments retaining the SMART/barcode end are amplifiable with the P5-SMART + N7xx primer pair; internal fragments (Nextera at both ends) are lost in the next PCR.
library_generation.product:
amplifiable (bead-proximal) fragment:
5'- AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT[CELL_BARCODE:12][UMI:8](T)30[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT... -3'
internal fragment (Nextera both ends, NOT amplified):
5'- ...AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT... -3'

library_generation.title: Library PCR (New-P5-SMART hybrid + Nextera N7xx)
library_generation.summary: PCR with the P5-SMART hybrid primer and a Nextera N7xx i7 primer adds P5 to the SMART end and the i7 index + P7 to the tagmented end.
library_generation.note: 12-cycle KAPA/Nextera PCR selectively amplifies barcode-bearing fragments into the final sequenceable library (400-800 bp).

library_sequencing.note: Spiked-in custom primer anneals to the P5/SMART bead handle so Read 1 begins at the cell barcode; reads the 12-nt cell barcode then the 8-nt UMI (20 bp; occasionally 21 bp by bead lot).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Custom Read 1 primer
          5'-GCCTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC----------------------->
                                                    (reads 12-nt CB + 8-nt UMI)
top    5'-...GCCTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC NNNNNNNNNNNN NNNNNNNN TTTTT...-3'
             |||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
bottom 3'-...CGGACAGGCGCCTTCGTCACCATAGTTGCGTCTCATG NNNNNNNNNNNN NNNNNNNN AAAAA...-5'
                                                  |cell barcode|| UMI  |

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8-nt i7 sample index off the tagmented (Nextera) end; on NovaSeq the protocol recommends dual-indexing to mitigate index hopping.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index primer
          5'-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC----------->
                                                (reads 8-nt i7 index)
top    5'-...CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC NNNNNNNN ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'
             ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
bottom 3'-...GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG NNNNNNNN TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'
                                               |i7 index|

library_sequencing.note: Anneals to the Nextera mosaic-end handle at the tagmented/P7 end and reads 50 bp (50-80 bp on NovaSeq) back into the cDNA insert.
library_sequencing.diagram:
<----------------------- (Read 2 reads into cDNA)
top    5'-...[cDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'
                    ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
Read2 primer     3'-GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG-5'
bottom 3'-...[cDNA']GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7']TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'

title: Seq-Well S3
description: Seq-Well S3 ("Second-Strand Synthesis") is a massively parallel, nanowell-array 3' single-cell RNA-seq method. Cells and uniquely barcoded poly(dT) capture beads are co-confined in a functionalized PDMS array sealed under a semipermeable polycarbonate membrane; captured mRNA is reverse-transcribed on-bead with a template-switch handle, then a randomly primed second-strand synthesis (Klenow, dN-SMRT primer) recovers cDNA molecules that failed template switching, appending the SMART handle to both ends. After whole-transcriptome PCR and Nextera XT tagmentation, the library is sequenced 3'-first: Read 1 reads the 12-nt cell barcode + 8-nt UMI off the bead handle, and Read 2 reads the cDNA insert.

reference.label: Second-Strand Synthesis-Based Massively Parallel scRNA-Seq Reveals Cellular States and Molecular Features of Human Inflammatory Skin Pathologies

original_publication.title: Second-Strand Synthesis-Based Massively Parallel scRNA-Seq Reveals Cellular States and Molecular Features of Human Inflammatory Skin Pathologies

throughput.summary: Nanowell-array platform (~85,000 wells per array); 10,000-15,000 cells loaded per array, up to 20 samples parallelized in a one-day experiment. The skin study recovered 38,274 high-quality single-cell transcriptomes across 19 biopsies.
throughput.cells: ~2,000-3,000 cells recovered per array (e.g. 2,682 PBMCs); 38,274 cells total across 19 skin biopsies
throughput.rna: Seq-Well S3: ~3,247 UMIs and ~1,402 genes per PBMC at ~47,000 reads/cell; up to 10-fold more transcripts and 5-fold more genes per cell than Seq-Well v1
throughput.dna: WTA product ~0.7-2 kb; tagmented sequencing library ~400-800 bp

publication.statistical_model: Library complexity modeled by multivariable linear regression of UMIs per cell on aligned reads x technique (nUMI ~ Intercept + B1*nReads + B2*Technique + B3*nReads*Technique); Mann-Whitney U tests for per-cell gene/transcript comparisons and Chi-square tests for gene/TCR detection frequencies.

other.label: Key innovation
other.value: Randomly primed second-strand synthesis (dN-SMRT + Klenow exo-) recovers cDNA that failed template switching, appending the SMART handle to both ends before WTA.

other.label: Array
other.value: Functionalized PDMS nanowell array (~85,000 wells) sealed with a hydroxylated polycarbonate membrane (10 nm pore)

other.label: Data availability
other.value: GEO: GSE150672; interactive data via the Alexandria Project (Broad single-cell portal)

other.label: Sequencing
other.value: NextSeq500 / NovaSeq; custom Read 1 primer spiked in; loaded at ~2.2 pM (NextSeq)

data_processing.summary: Reads were processed with the Drop-seq pipeline: BCL files were converted to FASTQ and demultiplexed by Nextera N700 index, aligned to hg19 with STAR, each read tagged with the 12-nt cell barcode and 8-nt UMI carried in Read 1, then grouped by barcode and collapsed by UMI to build a digital gene expression (DGE) matrix. Downstream ambient-RNA removal, doublet filtering, clustering and annotation were performed in Seurat/Scanpy.
data_processing.statistical_model: multivariable linear regression for library complexity (nUMI ~ Intercept + nReads + Technique + nReads*Technique); Mann-Whitney U for per-cell gene/transcript comparisons; Chi-square for gene/TCR detection frequencies
